en.extract:
The Sangihe whistler or Sangihe shrikethrush (Coracornis sanghirensis) is a species of bird in the family Pachycephalidae. It is endemic to Sangihe Island in Indonesia. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. Specifically, it lives in ridgetop forests with large trees and full canopy cover.
It is threatened by habitat loss. 
Originally, the Sangihe shrikethrush was described in the genus Pinarolestes. It was re-classified from the genus Colluricincla to Coracornis in 2013. Alternate names include the Sahengbalira shrike-thrush and Sangir whistler.

BirdLife Species Factsheet.

The '''Sangihe whistler''' or '''Sangihe shrikethrush''' ('''''Coracornis sanghirensis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pachycephalidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Sangir Island|Sangihe Island]] in Indonesia. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. Specifically, it lives in ridgetop forests with large trees and full [[Canopy (biology)|canopy]] cover.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Martin |first=Robert W. |last2=Collar |first2=Nigel J. |last3=Fauzen |first3=Panji |last4=Bashari |first4=Hanom |last5=Widyanto |first5=Adi |last6=Tasirin |first6=John S. |last7=Marsden |first7=Stuart J. |date=2022-09-05 |title=On the edge: habitat restoration priorities for three critically endangered bird species on Sangihe, Indonesia |journal=Restoration Ecology |volume=31 |issue=1 |doi=10.1111/rec.13770 |issn=1061-2971|doi-access=free }}</ref>

It is threatened by [[habitat loss]]. 

Originally, the Sangihe shrikethrush was described in the genus ''[[Pinarolestes]]''. It was re-classified from the genus ''[[Colluricincla]]'' to ''Coracornis'' in 2013.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archive-3-1-to-3-5/taxonomy-3-1-3-5/|title=Taxonomy 3.1-3.5 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-01-29}}</ref> Alternate names include the '''Sahengbalira shrike-thrush''' and '''Sangir whistler'''.
